title: ComfyUI Tutorial Series Ep 59: Qwen Edit Workflows for Smarter Image Edits
description:
In this video, I show how to use Qwen Edit workflows for smarter and more consistent image edits inside ComfyUI. Qwen Edit works similarly to Flux Kontext but often delivers better results with the right prompts.

You’ll learn how to:
- Set up Qwen Edit with the correct models (Q versions, CLIP, VAE, LoRA).
- Fix errors by updating ComfyUI and custom nodes.
- Build efficient workflows for editing, combining, inpainting, background replacement, and more.
- Use advanced editing techniques like ratio control, multi-image stitching, text replacement, transformations, and even product mockups.

👉 Whether you want to edit portraits, combine multiple images, change clothes, restore old photos, or create product mockups, this tutorial has practical step-by-step workflows you can follow right away.

Get the workflows and instructions from discord for free
Step 1: use this link to join if you didn't join already https://discord.gg/gggpkVgBf3
Step 2: then access directly this episode using this l

You can try to upscale it with the flux mania model or another realistic model to get more details and realism.  You can combine more than two images. For example, I can do three or four.  In this case, I used the stitch node to combine the man and the woman into one image, but stitch accepts only two images at once.  So I used another stitch to combine the dog image with the result of the first stitch, and I put the dog image down just to look better, but you can arrange them however you want.  And this is how the final image looks.  You can see we have combined all three images into one, and that resulting image then goes to the text encoder nodes.  Try to mention all the subjects and how each interacts with each other, and the result is this image.  I really like Gwen. I use it more and more every day. I like that it has better prompt understanding than flux.  Moving to the next workflow, I combined Gwen edit with in-painting nodes. All AI regenerate images similar to yours.  But we know that when the image is regenerated something gets lost. It is not perfectly identical, and sometimes realism is lost and so on.  So I like to use the in-paint node so I get the exact same image, and only a small portion is changed, the one I want to in-paint.  You need to install this node from manager if you do not have it already.  So let me show you how it works. First you load your image, then you need to select what you want to in-paint, so right click on the image and select open in mask editor.  Now here we can adjust the brush size and start painting the area we want to in-paint.  I usually do a little larger than the actual area I want to change, so if I want to add something on the head of this woman I can in-paint this area, and then I click save.  Now we have that area masked. So both the image and that mask go into this in-paint node. Make sure you check episode 42 on how to use this node better.  Then I prompt what I need to be changed in that area.  In this case I want to add a silver tiara. If I run the workflow I will get a generation with the woman with a tiara, but if we check the original image is there and only the in-paint area is changed, so I have exactly the same face.  This in-painting method is good to change, add, or remove things as long as the pose stays consistent so it can replace that area.  If you ask to add a tiara and then turn the head around or something it will not work because the workflow takes the original image and pays it back over the generation and only leaves the in-paint area visible.  So if it is too drastically changed it will not work well with in-paint.  Let's open the image again in the mask editor.  You can clear the selection and redo it if something does not work right or let's say I want to change the hair color instead so I will make a selection a little bigger than the hair then I save the mask.  I will adapt the prompt to say change the color of the hair to pink and let's run the workflow and look what we got. It did exactly what I asked for.  We have the same face and only that change is different and with this method you get better results than using quen edit alone which many times changes something in the face.
